destinations.summary: Volcanic cliffs and whitewashed villages offer some of the Mediterranean's most iconic sunset viewpoints.

viewpoints.name: Oia Castle Ruins Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: The most famous sunset spot on Santorini, perched above the caldera with sweeping views of the Aegean and the iconic blue-domed churches.
tips: Arrive 90 minutes before sunset to claim a spot.
Walk past the castle ruins for less crowded angles.
Stay after sunset for blue-hour photos.

viewpoints.name: Skaros Rock Trail Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: A short hike leads to the ruins of a medieval castle on a rocky promontory with dramatic caldera views away from the crowds.
tips: Wear sturdy shoes — the path is rocky.
Best visited early morning before tour groups arrive.
Bring water; there's little shade.

viewpoints.name: Fira Caldera Walk Lookout
viewpoints.description: The cliffside path between Fira and Firostefani offers continuous panoramic views of the volcano and whitewashed villages.
tips: Stop at a café midway for caldera-facing drinks.
The cable car down to the old port is nearby.
Evening lighting makes the white buildings glow.

viewpoints.name: Akrotiri Lighthouse Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: At the island's southwestern tip, this quiet lighthouse offers wide-open sea views and one of Santorini's best sunrise spots.
tips: Rent a car or ATV — public transport is limited.
Combine with Red Beach visit nearby.
Windy in winter — bring a jacket.

viewpoints.name: Profitis Ilias Monastery Summit
viewpoints.description: Santorini's highest point provides a 360° panorama of the entire island, the caldera, and neighboring islands on clear days.
tips: Visibility is best on cloudless days.
The monastery grounds are peaceful for a short visit.
Drive up — the road is steep but paved.

viewpoints.name: Megalochori Village Rooftops
viewpoints.description: This traditional village on the caldera's edge offers charming alleyways and rooftop terraces with quieter sunset views than Oia.
tips: Try a local winery before sunset.
Explore the bell tower area for elevated views.
Far fewer tourists than Oia or Fira.

destinations.summary: Ancient Incan citadel surrounded by cloud forest peaks with world-renowned mountain viewpoints.

viewpoints.name: Guardhouse (Caretaker's Hut) Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: The iconic postcard view of Machu Picchu — the entire citadel spread below with Huayna Picchu rising dramatically behind.
tips: Enter at opening time for mist-free photos.
The first viewpoint after entering the site.
Tripods require a special permit.

viewpoints.name: Huayna Picchu Summit
viewpoints.description: A steep climb to the peak behind the citadel rewards you with a bird's-eye view of the entire archaeological site and Urubamba Valley.
tips: Book Huayna Picchu tickets months in advance.
Not recommended if you fear heights.
Allow 2–3 hours for the round trip.

viewpoints.name: Sun Gate (Inti Punku)
viewpoints.description: The traditional end of the Inca Trail opens to a breathtaking first glimpse of Machu Picchu from above — especially magical at dawn.
tips: Accessible without doing the full Inca Trail.
About 1.5–2 hours hike from the citadel.
First light hits the ruins beautifully from here.

viewpoints.name: Machu Picchu Mountain Summit
viewpoints.description: Higher and less crowded than Huayna Picchu, this summit offers expansive views of the citadel, surrounding peaks, and cloud forest.
tips: Easier to get tickets than Huayna Picchu.
Bring rain gear — weather changes quickly.
The trail is longer but less steep in sections.

viewpoints.name: Putucusi Mountain Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: Across the river from Aguas Calientes, this lesser-known climb offers a unique side-angle view of Machu Picchu mountain.
tips: Check trail status — sections may be closed.
Start from Aguas Calientes town.
Bring gloves for ladder sections.

viewpoints.name: Mandor Gardens River View
viewpoints.description: A peaceful botanical garden along the Urubamba River with mountain views and orchids — a relaxing contrast to the citadel crowds.
tips: Walkable from Aguas Calientes in about 1 hour.
Great for acclimatization day before visiting the citadel.
Small entrance fee applies.

destinations.summary: Dramatic limestone cliffs, pastel villages, and Mediterranean vistas define Italy's most scenic coastline.

viewpoints.name: Sentiero degli Dei (Path of the Gods)
viewpoints.description: This legendary hiking trail between Bomerano and Nocelle offers continuous jaw-dropping views of the coastline, Capri, and terraced hillsides.
tips: Start early to avoid midday heat.
Wear proper hiking shoes.
Bring plenty of water — limited shops on trail.

viewpoints.name: Ravello — Villa Rufolo Gardens Terrace
viewpoints.description: Terraced gardens perched high above the coast provide one of the Amalfi's most romantic views — Wagner called it the closest thing to paradise.
tips: Visit Villa Cimbrone nearby for another stunning terrace.
Concert season runs June through September.
Combine with lunch in Ravello's piazza.

viewpoints.name: Positano Beach Overlook
viewpoints.description: From the main coastal road above town, Positino's colorful stacked houses cascade down to the beach — one of Italy's most photographed views.
tips: Bus stop on SS163 offers the classic angle.
Visit early morning for photos without crowds.
Walk down to the beach for a different perspective.

viewpoints.name: Amalfi Cathedral Steps Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: The grand staircase leading to the Duomo di Amalfi frames mountain and sea views while showcasing Arab-Norman architecture.
tips: Visit the cathedral crypt and museum.
Try local limoncello at nearby shops.
Less crowded before 10 AM.

viewpoints.name: Praiano — Marina di Praia Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: A quieter alternative to Positano, this fishing village cove is framed by towering cliffs and offers intimate coastal views.
tips: Dine at a cliffside restaurant at sunset.
Accessible by local SITA bus.
Kayak rentals available in summer.

viewpoints.name: Capo di Sorrento Ruins View
viewpoints.description: Roman villa ruins on a cliff edge near Sorrento provide historical context and sweeping Gulf of Naples views including Vesuvius.
tips: Also known as Baths of Queen Joan.
Free to visit the exterior ruins.
Combine with Sorrento old town walk.

destinations.summary: Adventure capital of the world, set on Lake Wakatipu with Remarkables mountain backdrops at every turn.

viewpoints.name: Skyline Queenstown Gondola Lookout
viewpoints.description: Ride the gondola to Bob's Peak for panoramic views of Queenstown, Lake Wakatipu, and The Remarkables mountain range.
tips: Book luge rides for added fun.
Restaurant at top for sunset dinner.
Return after dark for city lights view.

viewpoints.name: Ben Lomond Track Summit
viewpoints.description: A challenging day hike rewards you with 360° views of Lake Wakatipu, Queenstown, and surrounding Southern Alps peaks.
tips: Start from Skyline access road.
Allow 6–8 hours round trip.
Check weather — exposed above treeline.

viewpoints.name: Queenstown Hill (Te Tapu-nui)
viewpoints.description: A 2–3 hour loop through pine forest leads to the 'Basket of Dreams' sculpture and excellent lake and mountain views.
tips: Free and less touristy than Skyline.
Steep sections near the top.
Great for a half-day outing.

viewpoints.name: Glenorchy Lagoon Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: The mirror-still waters of the lagoon reflect the Earnslaw Range — a Lord of the Rings filming location and photographer's dream.
tips: 45-minute drive from Queenstown — worth the trip.
Calm mornings give best reflections.
Continue to Glenorchy village for lunch.

viewpoints.name: Lake Wakatipu Shore Walk — Frankton Arm
viewpoints.description: A flat lakeside trail with uninterrupted views of The Remarkables reflected in the crystal-clear water.
tips: Rent a bike for the full trail.
Stop at Frankton Beach for a swim in summer.
Clear days offer mirror reflections.

viewpoints.name: Moke Lake Overlook
viewpoints.description: A secluded alpine lake surrounded by tussock hills offers a peaceful escape with mountain views and excellent stargazing.
tips: Gravel road access — check conditions.
Department of Conservation campsite nearby.
Bring warm layers even in summer.

destinations.summary: Heart of the Canadian Rockies with turquoise lakes, towering peaks, and world-class scenic drives.

viewpoints.name: Lake Louise Lakeshore Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: The vivid turquoise waters of Lake Louise framed by Victoria Glacier and the Fairmont Chateau — one of Canada's most iconic views.
tips: Arrive before 7 AM to avoid crowds and get parking.
Canoe rentals available in summer.
Hike to Lake Agnes Tea House for elevated views.

viewpoints.name: Sulphur Mountain Summit (Banff Gondola)
viewpoints.description: The Banff Gondola takes you to 2,281m with boardwalk views of six mountain ranges, the Bow Valley, and Banff townsite.
tips: Book tickets online in advance.
Cosmic Ray Station at the summit is historic.
Dine at Sky Bistro for mountain views.

viewpoints.name: Moraine Lake Rockpile Trail
viewpoints.description: A short climb up the rockpile reveals the famous 'Twenty Dollar View' — Moraine Lake surrounded by ten jagged peaks.
tips: Road closes in winter — check seasonal access.
Shuttle reservation required in peak season.
Only 300m walk to the top viewpoint.

viewpoints.name: Surprise Corner Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: A roadside pullout revealing the Fairmont Banff Springs castle hotel framed by Rundle Mountain and the Bow River.
tips: Easy walk from Banff town center.
Beautiful in all seasons.
Combine with Bow Falls visit nearby.

viewpoints.name: Peyto Lake Bow Summit
viewpoints.description: From Bow Summit, gaze down at Peyto Lake's distinctive wolf-head shape and impossible blue glacial water.
tips: On Icefields Parkway — combine with Columbia Icefield.
Short paved trail to viewing platform.
Color is most vivid on sunny summer days.

viewpoints.name: Tunnel Mountain Summit Trail
viewpoints.description: Banff's smallest summit hike offers rewarding views of the town, Bow River, and surrounding peaks in under 2 hours.
tips: Trailhead near Banff Centre.
Popular with locals for evening runs.
Watch for wildlife — bears active in season.

destinations.summary: The City of Light offers iconic monuments, river vistas, and rooftop panoramas at every corner.

viewpoints.name: Eiffel Tower — Trocadéro Gardens
viewpoints.description: The classic frontal view of the Eiffel Tower from across the Seine, with fountains and gardens framing the iron lattice icon.
tips: Tower sparkles every hour on the hour after dark.
Very crowded — visit early morning for photos.
Metro: Trocadéro station.

viewpoints.name: Montmartre — Sacré-Cœur Steps
viewpoints.description: From the steps of Sacré-Cœur, enjoy sweeping views over Paris's rooftops — best at sunset with street performers and artists.
tips: Watch for pickpockets in crowded areas.
Explore Place du Tertre for local art.
Funicular available if you skip the stairs.

viewpoints.name: Arc de Triomphe Rooftop
viewpoints.description: Climb to the top of the Arc for a unique perspective down the twelve radiating avenues including the Champs-Élysées.
tips: Buy tickets online to skip the queue.
284 steps — no elevator.
La Défense skyline visible on clear days.

viewpoints.name: Seine River — Pont des Arts
viewpoints.description: The pedestrian bridge offers romantic views of the Louvre, Institut de France, and river traffic on the Seine.
tips: Perfect for a picnic at sunset.
Continue to Île de la Cité nearby.
Bateau-mouche cruises depart nearby.

viewpoints.name: Notre-Dame Towers Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: Climb the north tower for close-up gargoyle views and a panorama of Paris including the Eiffel Tower and Pantheon.
tips: Check reopening status after restoration.
422 steps to the top.
Book timed entry in advance.

viewpoints.name: Parc des Buttes-Chaumont
viewpoints.description: A local-favorite park with a temple perched on a rocky island, offering unexpected elevated views over northeast Paris.
tips: Less touristy than central spots.
Great for a morning jog or picnic.
Temple de la Sybille is the highlight.

destinations.summary: A dazzling blend of ancient temples and neon skylines with incredible city and mountain viewpoints.

viewpoints.name: Tokyo Skytree Observation Deck
viewpoints.description: At 634 meters, Japan's tallest structure offers breathtaking 360° views stretching to Mount Fuji on clear days.
tips: Clear winter days best for Fuji views.
Tembo Deck (350m) and Galleria (450m) available.
Book Fast Skytree Ticket online.

viewpoints.name: Shibuya Sky Observatory
viewpoints.description: An open-air rooftop 230m above Shibuya Crossing offers dramatic cityscape views and the famous scramble intersection below.
tips: Sunset slots sell out fast — book ahead.
Glass floor sections for thrill seekers.
Best night views in Tokyo.

viewpoints.name: Tokyo Tower Main Observatory
viewpoints.description: The iconic red-and-white tower inspired by the Eiffel Tower provides classic Tokyo skyline views from its two observation decks.
tips: Less crowded than Skytree.
FootTown shopping at the base.
Illuminated beautifully at night.

viewpoints.name: Meiji Shrine Forest Clearing
viewpoints.description: A peaceful 70-hectare forest in the heart of Tokyo surrounds the Meiji Shrine — a serene contrast to the city's energy.
tips: Visit early morning for tranquility.
Free entry to the forest and outer shrine.
Combine with Harajuku shopping nearby.

viewpoints.name: Odaiba — Rainbow Bridge Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: The futuristic waterfront district offers views of Rainbow Bridge, Tokyo Bay, and a miniature Statue of Liberty.
tips: Take the Yurikamome line for scenic approach.
TeamLab Borderless nearby.
Bridge illuminations after sunset.

viewpoints.name: Mount Takao Summit
viewpoints.description: Just an hour from central Tokyo, this sacred mountain offers hiking trails and summit views of Tokyo and Mount Fuji.
tips: Cable car available partway up.
Autumn foliage is spectacular.
Try local soba at the summit.

destinations.summary: Sun-kissed beaches, Portuguese heritage, and lush Western Ghats viewpoints along India's tropical coast.

viewpoints.name: Fort Aguada Lighthouse Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: A 17th-century Portuguese fort perched above Aguada Beach offers panoramic views of the Arabian Sea and Candolim coastline.
tips: Visit the lighthouse for elevated views.
Combine with Sinquerim Beach below.
Entry to fort area is free.

viewpoints.name: Chapora Fort (Dil Chahta Hai Point)
viewpoints.description: Made famous by a Bollywood film, these fort ruins overlook Vagator Beach, Ozran Beach, and the meeting of river and sea.
tips: Short uphill walk from parking.
Best sunset spot in North Goa.
Wear comfortable shoes for rocky paths.

viewpoints.name: Dudhsagar Falls Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: One of India's tallest waterfalls cascades 310 meters through lush jungle — a milky-white spectacle best seen in monsoon.
tips: Jeep safari from Collem village required.
Monsoon (June–Sept) for fullest flow.
Swimming allowed at base in season.

viewpoints.name: Fontainhas Latin Quarter Walk
viewpoints.description: Panaji's colorful Portuguese-era quarter offers charming narrow streets, painted houses, and glimpses of the Mandovi River.
tips: Start at Chapel of St Sebastian.
Visit the Mario Gallery for local art.
Best light for photos before 10 AM.

viewpoints.name: Cabo de Rama Fort Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: One of Goa's oldest forts sits on a cliff edge in South Goa with sweeping views of the Arabian Sea and secluded beaches below.
tips: Much quieter than North Goa forts.
Small chapel inside the fort.
Combine with Agonda or Palolem beach.

viewpoints.name: Divar Island Ferry Viewpoint
viewpoints.description: A peaceful island on the Mandovi River with village life, hilltop churches, and views back toward Old Goa.
tips: Take ferry from Old Goa or Ribandar.
Rent a scooter to explore the island.
Our Lady of Compassion hilltop church has best views.

destinations.summary: The Pink City dazzles with royal palaces, hilltop forts, and desert-edge viewpoints across Rajasthan.

viewpoints.name: Amer Fort (Amber Fort) Ramparts
viewpoints.description: This magnificent hilltop fort offers views over Maota Lake and the Aravalli hills from its ornate ramparts and Sheesh Mahal.
tips: Arrive at opening for fewer crowds.
Elephant rides available but walking is recommended.
Sound and light show in evening.

viewpoints.name: Nahargarh Fort Sunset Point
viewpoints.description: Perched on the Aravalli ridge, Nahargarh offers the best panoramic view of Jaipur's pink cityscape — especially at sunset.
tips: Padao Restaurant at fort for sunset dining.
Combine with Jaigarh Fort nearby.
Road accessible by car or bike.

viewpoints.name: Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds) Facade
viewpoints.description: The iconic honeycomb facade with 953 windows was built for royal women to observe street festivals — best viewed from across the road.
tips: Morning sun illuminates the pink facade.
Café across street for elevated photo angle.
Climb inside for street-level views through windows.

viewpoints.name: Jantar Mantar Observatory Terrace
viewpoints.description: UNESCO-listed astronomical instruments from the 18th century include the world's largest stone sundial — a unique elevated perspective.
tips: Hire a guide to understand the instruments.
Adjacent to City Palace — combine visits.
Less crowded before 10 AM.

viewpoints.name: Jal Mahal (Water Palace) Lake View
viewpoints.description: The Water Palace appears to float in Man Sagar Lake with Nahargarh hills behind — a magical sight especially during monsoon when the lake is full.
tips: View from roadside promenade — palace interior closed.
Lake full during and after monsoon.
Birdwatching spot in winter.

viewpoints.name: Galta Ji (Monkey Temple) Canyon View
viewpoints.description: A hidden temple complex in a rocky canyon with natural springs, sacred pools, and surprising valley views away from tourist crowds.
tips: Watch belongings around monkeys.
Wear modest clothing for temple visit.
Hike up to Sun Temple for best views.
